summary:
# Verification Report

**Verdict:** FAIL
**Claims reviewed:** 26
**Sources reviewed:** 12
**Open agenda items:** 7

## Findings
1. [HIGH] Major tech companies like IBM, Salesforce, Google, and Meta are prone to AI-driven layoffs as automation progresses. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
2. [HIGH] Logistics companies such as Amazon, UPS, and FedEx are expanding automation to displace traditional manual labor roles. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
3. [MEDIUM] AI management roles are becoming crucial as customer service sectors shift towards automated platforms. — Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
4. [HIGH] The US administration in 2026 continues to evolve workforce policies focused on managing AI-induced displacement. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
5. [MEDIUM] Historical cycles, such as the 1980s manufacturing shift, inform modern policy responses to technological changes. — Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
